stem: Evaluate the following integrals. Show your work. Simplify and box your answers.
Evaluate the following integrals. Show your work. Simplify and box your answers.

parts.question_text: $(10 \text{ points}) \text{ Find the arc length of the curve } y = \frac{1}{16}e^{2x} + e^{-2x} \text{ for } 0 \le x \le 1.$

$(10 \text{ points}) \text{ The } \textbf{position} \text{ of a particle at time } t \text{ is given by } s(t) = t^3 - 2t^2 - 4t + 20. \text{ Find the total distance travelled by the particle from } t = 0 \text{ to } t = 3.$

stem: The region below $y = \sin^2 x$, above the $x$-axis, between $x = 0$ and $x = \pi$ is rotated about the line $x = -\pi$ to generate a solid of revolution.

parts.label: a
parts.question_text: $(5 \text{ points}) \text{ Write down an integral equal to the volume of the resulting solid of revolution. Do not compute the integral yet. }$
parts.diagram_description: A coordinate system with x-axis from approximately -π to π and y-axis from 0 to 1. A shaded region is shown between the x-axis and a curve that appears to be y = sin²(x) from x = 0 to x = π. The curve rises from 0, reaches a maximum of 1 at x = π/2, and returns to 0 at x = π. There is a vertical dashed line at x = -π to indicate the axis of rotation.

parts.label: b
parts.question_text: $(5 \text{ points}) \text{ Compute the integral to determine the volume of the solid of revolution. Give your answer in exact form or as a decimal number with at least four significant digits. }$

stem: The graph of a function $f(t)$ is shown. Use it to answer the following questions.

parts.label: a
parts.question_text: $(5 \text{ points}) \text{ Approximate the } \textbf{average value} \text{ of this function over the interval } [0,2] \text{ using Simpson's Rule with } n = 4 \text{ subintervals. }$
parts.diagram_description: A graph of function f(t) on a coordinate grid. The x-axis ranges from 0 to 2 with marked intervals. The y-axis ranges from -4 to 5. The curve starts at approximately (0, 2), rises to a maximum of about (0.5, 5), decreases through approximately (1, 0), reaches a minimum of about (1.5, -3.5), and then increases to about (2, 4). The curve is smooth and continuous.

parts.label: b
parts.question_text: $(5 \text{ points}) \text{ Define a new function: } g(x) = \int_{x}^{2x} f(t) dt, \text{ where } f \text{ is the function in the graph above. Compute } g'(0.5)$

stem: Consider the region bounded above by the graph of $y = \frac{1}{1 + x^2}$, bounded below by the graph of $y = \frac{-1}{1 + x^2}$, bounded on the left by the $y$ axis, and with no bound on the right.

parts.label: a
parts.question_text: $(5 \text{ points}) \text{ Find the area of this region. If it's finite, compute its value. If it's infinite, show why. Show all steps and limit computations in either case. }$
parts.diagram_description: A coordinate system showing a symmetric region about the x-axis. The upper boundary is the curve y = 1/(1+x²) starting from the y-axis and extending to the right. The lower boundary is the curve y = -1/(1+x²) extending to the right. The region is shaded gray and appears to be bounded on the left by the y-axis (at x = 0) and extends infinitely to the right. Both curves approach the x-axis asymptotically.

parts.label: b
parts.question_text: $(5 \text{ points}) \text{ Find the } \bar{x} \text{ coordinate of the center of mass of this region. If it's finite, compute its value. If it's infinite, show why. Show all steps and limit computations in either case. }$

stem:
A part of an elevated freeway is 30 meters long and has half elliptical cross sections, as shown below. The width at the top of the cross section (the horizontal axis of the ellipse) is 4 meters. The height in the middle of the cross section (half of the vertical axis of the ellipse) is 1 meter. The lowest point of the section is 5 meters above the ground.

The structure is made of concrete which was pumped from the ground level up, in liquid state. The mass density of concrete is 2,400 kg per cubic meter. The gravitational acceleration is 9.8 m/sec². 

SET UP (do NOT evaluate) an integral equal to the WORK that was done to pump the concrete up to make the section of the structure. The work done to make the support column should be ignored.

Recall that the equation of an ellipse centered at the origin is $\frac{x^2}{a^2} + \frac{y^2}{b^2} = 1$, where $a$ and $b$ are half of the horizontal and vertical axes of the ellipse, respectively.

parts.diagram_description: Two diagrams: (1) A 3D perspective drawing of an elevated freeway section 30 meters long with a curved top cross-section shaped like a half ellipse. (2) A 2D cross-section diagram showing the elliptical shape. The cross-section shows a coordinate system with the x-axis horizontal spanning from -2 to 2 (total width 4 meters), and the y-axis vertical. The top of the half ellipse is at height 1 meter. The diagram indicates that the lowest point of the section is 5 meters above the ground. The diagram is labeled to show the 2-2 width dimensions and the 5 meter height above ground.

parts.question_text: $(10 \text{ points}) \text{ Find the solution of the initial value problem } y' = (y^2 + 4)^{3/2} \ln x \text{ with } y(1) = 2. \text{ Leave your answer as an implicit equation involving the variables } y \text{ and } x.$

stem: At this time, the population of Mountainstan is 100 million people. The natural growth rate (births minus deaths) is 2% of population per year. Each year 1 million people emigrate from Mountainstan to other countries looking for better economic opportunities. Assume no people move from other countries to Mountainstan.

parts.label: a
parts.question_text: $(4 \text{ points}) \text{ Let } y = y(t) \text{ be the population of Mountainstan, in millions, } t \text{ years from now. Write down a differential equation for } y. \text{ Do NOT solve the equation yet. }$

parts.label: b
parts.question_text: $(6 \text{ points}) \text{ Assuming that these trends will be stable for a long time, determine how many years from now the population will reach 150 million. Give your answer as a decimal number with at least 4 significant digits. }$
